name: accessibility-wcag
description: Enforce WCAG 2.2 accessibility standards. Use when creating UI components, reviewing frontend code, or when accessibility issues are detected. Covers semantic HTML, ARIA, keyboard navigation, and color contrast.
capabilities: Validates semantic HTML element usage for proper document structure
Checks image alt text requirements and decorative image handling
Ensures keyboard accessibility and focus management in components
Verifies color contrast ratios meet WCAG AA (4.5:1) standards
Validates ARIA roles, states, and properties for assistive technology
Reviews form label associations and error message accessibility
author: Doyajin174
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only accessibility guidelines. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or command execution. All 49 static findings are false positives from markdown code fences and documentation references being misinterpreted as security issues.
valueStatement: Many websites fail accessibility standards, excluding users with disabilities. This skill ensures all UI components meet WCAG 2.2 requirements including semantic HTML, ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, and color contrast ratios.
limitations: Cannot test actual screen reader output on live pages
Cannot automatically measure color contrast values without visual analysis
Cannot verify accessibility through browser-based testing
Does not replace manual accessibility testing with assistive technologies

useCases.title: Build accessible components
useCases.description: Create new React, Vue, or HTML components following WCAG 2.2 guidelines for full accessibility compliance.

useCases.title: Review code for accessibility
useCases.description: Identify and fix accessibility issues in pull requests before merging to main branch.

useCases.title: Audit existing interfaces
useCases.description: Evaluate current web applications for WCAG 2.2 compliance and document accessibility gaps.

promptTemplates.title: Basic accessibility check
promptTemplates.scenario: Check a component for WCAG compliance
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this React component for WCAG 2.2 accessibility issues. Check for semantic HTML, keyboard accessibility, ARIA attributes, and color contrast. List any violations and provide fixed code.

promptTemplates.title: Form accessibility review
promptTemplates.prompt: Check this form implementation for accessibility compliance. Verify all inputs have proper labels, error messages are announced to screen readers, and focus management works correctly.

promptTemplates.title: Modal dialog audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this modal dialog implementation. Ensure it has proper role='dialog', aria-modal, keyboard trapping, focus management, and escape key support per WCAG 2.2 guidelines.

promptTemplates.title: Color contrast analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the color scheme in this stylesheet. Calculate contrast ratios for all text colors against their backgrounds. Flag any that fail WCAG AA (4.5:1) or AAA (7:1) requirements.

bestPractices: Use semantic HTML elements (<button>, <nav>, <main>) instead of <div> with ARIA attributes when native elements exist
Ensure all interactive elements are keyboard accessible with visible focus indicators that do not rely on color alone
Test with keyboard-only navigation and screen readers before deployment to catch practical accessibility issues
antiPatterns: Using <div> with onClick instead of <button> elements - removes native keyboard support and focus management
Removing outline:focus styles without providing alternative visible focus indicators
Relying solely on color to convey information without text labels, icons, or other non-color indicators

faq.question: What WCAG level does this skill enforce?
faq.answer: This skill enforces WCAG 2.2 Level AA requirements as the minimum standard for all components.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all frontend frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes. The accessibility principles apply to React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, and plain HTML/CSS projects.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for mobile app accessibility?
faq.answer: Most guidelines transfer to React Native and Flutter. Native iOS/Android require platform-specific accessibility APIs.

faq.question: Does this skill store any user data?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based skill with no data storage, network calls, or file system access capabilities.

faq.question: How do I verify accessibility beyond this skill?
faq.answer: Use axe-core, Lighthouse, or WAVE for automated testing. Complete manual testing with keyboard and screen readers.

faq.question: How does this compare to ESLint accessibility plugins?
faq.answer: This skill catches broader design patterns and provides fixes. Use eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y for build-time automated enforcement.
